explanation: This command simulates the execution of msdt.exe with an answer file, which is the core behavior of the Sigma rule. It uses a standard flag format (-af) and a hardcoded path to the XML file.
This command uses the equivalent /af flag instead of -af and includes double backslashes for escaping. It still represents the same behavior as the Sigma rule.
This command uses single quotes instead of double quotes to enclose the path, which is a safe mutation technique and still represents the same behavior.
This command omits the quotes around the path. Since the path does not contain spaces, it is still valid and represents the same behavior.
This command uses a mix of single and double backslashes for path normalization, which is a safe mutation technique and still represents the same behavior.
